name: Tasting the Landscape
description:
When does food become a pest, and who decides?

Some cultures view grasshoppers and locusts (Cavallette in Italian) as symbols of plague and famine, while others consume them as an everyday protein source. Join artist Giulia Berra for a deep dive into her exhibit ‘Cavallette (Grasshoppers),’ as she unpacks connections between the apparent extremes of food and famine and people and pests. She will explore her artistic method based on natural leftovers and residual elements, focusing on artworks made from insects or food waste. This will be followed by a virtual table where participants can share their experiences, as people do at dinner.

name:
Educator Workshop: 
Interdisciplinary Thinking In Practice
description:
Click here to [register]! 

Learning is often presented as distinct, neatly defined subjects.
Interdisciplinary thinking integrates them, opening up a word that is complex, interconnected, and full of surprises!

The Educator Workshop is a full-day immersive workshop for educators from across the city, to explore new ideas, think across disciplines, and reimagine learning together. Because the future of education is becoming increasingly interdisciplinary.

What to expect?
> Experience our exhibition-season CALORIE, where ideas from science, art, design, and society intersect.
> Unpack interdisciplinarity through hands-on activities and reflective discussions.
> Explore NCERT topics through a fresh lens.
> Collaborate in small groups to design practical ideas for sparking interdisciplinarity in the classroom. 
> Present your insights in a lively pitching session.

Who is this for?
Educators and teachers across subjects (Classes 7–12) who value curiosity, inquiry, and exploring ideas across disciplines.


Come, be a learner for a day.
Connect with fellow educators.
Return inspired—with practical tools and renewed energy for teaching.

Click here to [register]! 

[register]: https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSdbACchT1wSVyhPTilwpQG84SMcXM2kEq2hbpp2t9xjj5XoMg/viewform

name: The Jazz Kitchen: Jazz, Poetry & Songwriting
description:
What happens when food, poetry and jazz meet?

Jazz poetry is a literary genre in which poetry is informed by jazz music. That is, the poet responds to and writes about jazz. It encompasses a variety of forms, rhythms, and sounds. But writing about jazz poetry, as they say, is like dancing about architecture. This session explores that paradox as the MoonArra quartet performs poetry with jazz's rhythmic and improvisational techniques. Participants can engage in the process of creating a basic blues song on the theme of food, which will be set to rhythm and accompaniment by the quartet.

name: The Frugal Innovation Forum : Paving the Way for Sustainable Future
description:
The Frugal Innovation Forum is an international gathering exploring how frugal innovation can address major global challenges through resource-efficient, inclusive, and human-centred approaches.

Bringing together experts from France and India, the forum examines how constraints and resource limitations can drive creativity, particularly in areas such as technology, sustainability, healthcare, digital transformation, and public innovation, including emerging challenges such as the rise of artificial intelligence.

The programme includes keynote speeches, panel discussions, workshops, and interactive sessions with innovators, researchers, and policymakers, hosted at the Science Gallery Bengaluru and the Alliance Française de Bangalore.

Held in the framework of the France–India Year of Innovation 2026, the forum focuses on strengthening Indo-French collaboration in response to evolving social, environmental, and technological challenges.

Full programme here - [Link]

[Link]: https://www.institutfrancaisindia.in/event/the-frugal-innovation-forum/

name: Eat, Read, Think
description:
Eat, Read, Think is a reading circle run by Science Gallery Bengaluru, bringing together curious minds to explore ideas through literature and conversation. Readings will be themed around the current exhibition season, CALORIE, exploring humanity's fraught and intimate relationship with food through readings inspired by the many exhibits present throughout the gallery. Eat, Read, Think welcomes young people from all walks of life to gather for conversations that connect science, culture and society.

The seventh edition of the circle will read 'Watercress' an award winning childrens book written by Andrea Wang, illustrated by Jason Chin. 

Come for community stay for snacks!

name: Forbidden Desire: Sugar Blowing Workshop
description:
Sugar is a material full of contrasts, a sweet delight and a forbidden indulgence. Inspired by artist Jade Armstrong’s original concept, this workshop explores the delicate balance between beauty, sweetness, and the fleeting nature of life.

In this workshop, Chef Mithuran Chakravarthi will guide the participants to learn and to create intricate sugar-blown sculptures, each capturing a single breath and symbolising both the passage of time and the fragility of life. As these sculptures slowly melt into syrupy residue, they reflect sugar’s transformation from a prized luxury to an overconsumed treat.

This hands-on experience encourages reflection on desire, decay, and consumption.

name: By The Clock: Exploring the Intersection of Labour and Time in CALORIE
description:
In the past, labour determined time, and now time determines everything! What role did industrialisation, food practices, and social systems play in dividing our days into shifts, breaks, and “time off”?

This walkthrough traces key moments in the evolution of time, from task-based rhythms to standardised hours that organise modern labour. 

Through CALORIE, we will explore how time structures not just labour, but also rest, meals, and everyday routines.
